req.title: Hr-Admin updates onboarding-task
req.statement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to update a onboarding-task through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates that an authenticated hr-admin can update an onboarding-task via UI, that mandatory fields are validated, and that an audit record is written on success. I selected: (1) a positive EP test covering the happy-path update with all valid mandatory fields, verifying HTTP 200/201, entity ID in response, and audit record creation; (2) a negative NEG/DT test submitting the form with a blank mandatory field to confirm HTTP 400 and inline validation error; (3) a boundary BVA test setting a text field to exactly its maximum allowed length (255 chars) to verify the system accepts the boundary value; (4) a negative NEG test exercising the 401 unauthenticated pathway; (5) a state-transition STC test verifying the task moves from 'pending' to 'completed' status and the audit log reflects the transition.

tests.name: test_hr_admin_updates_onboarding_task_valid_fields
preconditions: A pre-existing onboarding-task with known ID exists (created via <fixture: existing_onboarding_task>)
actions: Fill all mandatory fields with valid representative values
expected: Response body contains the updated entity ID
Onboarding-task record reflects new values in the database
An audit record is written for the update action
trace: R-WEB-026: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_hr_admin_updates_onboarding_task_valid_fields(
    hr_admin_page,          # <fixture> authenticated Playwright page
    existing_onboarding_task,  # <fixture> returns {"id": str, "title": str}
    audit_log_client,       # <fixture> thin wrapper around audit API
):
    task_id = existing_onboarding_task["id"]
    page = hr_admin_page

    page.goto(f"/onboarding-tasks/{task_id}/edit")
    page.fill("[data-testid='task-title']", "Updated Task Title")
    page.fill("[data-testid='task-description']", "Updated description text")
    page.select_option("[data-testid='task-status']", "in_progress")

    with page.expect_response(
        lambda r: f"/onboarding-tasks/{task_id}" in r.url
        and r.request.method in ("PUT", "PATCH", "POST")
    ) as resp_info:
        page.click("[data-testid='submit-btn']")

    response = resp_info.value
    assert response.status in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201, got {response.status}"
    body = response.json()
    assert str(body.get("id")) == str(task_id)

    expect(page.locator("[data-testid='success-toast']")).to_be_visible()

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=task_id, action="update")
    assert len(audit_entries) >= 1, "Audit record not written after update"

tests.name: test_hr_admin_update_onboarding_task_missing_mandatory_field_returns_400
preconditions: A pre-existing onboarding-task with known ID exists (created via <fixture: existing_onboarding_task>)
expected: Inline validation error message visible for the 'title' field
Onboarding-task record is NOT updated in the database
trace: R-WEB-026: mandatory fields must be validated
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_hr_admin_update_onboarding_task_missing_mandatory_field_returns_400(
    hr_admin_page,
    existing_onboarding_task,
    audit_log_client,
):
    task_id = existing_onboarding_task["id"]
    original_title = existing_onboarding_task["title"]
    page = hr_admin_page

    page.goto(f"/onboarding-tasks/{task_id}/edit")
    page.fill("[data-testid='task-title']", "")

    with page.expect_response(
        lambda r: f"/onboarding-tasks" in r.url
        and r.request.method in ("PUT", "PATCH", "POST")
    ) as resp_info:
        page.click("[data-testid='submit-btn']")

    response = resp_info.value
    assert response.status == 400, f"Expected 400, got {response.status}"

    error_locator = page.locator("[data-testid='field-error-title']")
    expect(error_locator).to_be_visible()
    expect(error_locator).to_contain_text(
        <helper_fn: get_validation_message>("title", "required")
    )

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=task_id, action="update")
    assert len(audit_entries) == 0, "Audit record must NOT be written on validation failure"

tests.name: test_hr_admin_update_onboarding_task_title_at_max_boundary_length
preconditions: A pre-existing onboarding-task with known ID exists (created via <fixture: existing_onboarding_task>)
Maximum allowed length for 'title' field is 255 characters (per data dictionary)
actions: Fill 'title' field with a string of exactly 255 characters
expected: Response body contains the updated entity ID
trace: R-WEB-026: mandatory field boundary at max length
R-WEB-026: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

MAX_TITLE_LEN = 255

def test_hr_admin_update_onboarding_task_title_at_max_boundary_length(
    hr_admin_page,
    existing_onboarding_task,
    audit_log_client,
):
    task_id = existing_onboarding_task["id"]
    max_title = "A" * MAX_TITLE_LEN
    page = hr_admin_page

    page.goto(f"/onboarding-tasks/{task_id}/edit")
    page.fill("[data-testid='task-title']", max_title)

    with page.expect_response(
        lambda r: f"/onboarding-tasks/{task_id}" in r.url
        and r.request.method in ("PUT", "PATCH", "POST")
    ) as resp_info:
        page.click("[data-testid='submit-btn']")

    response = resp_info.value
    assert response.status in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201 at max boundary, got {response.status}"
    body = response.json()
    assert str(body.get("id")) == str(task_id)
    assert body.get("title") == max_title, "Stored title must equal the 255-char boundary value"

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=task_id, action="update")
    assert len(audit_entries) >= 1, "Audit record not written for boundary update"

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_update_onboarding_task
preconditions: No active session / user is unauthenticated
A pre-existing onboarding-task ID is known
actions: Attempt a direct PUT/PATCH request to /onboarding-tasks/{task_id} without auth token
expected: Response body contains authentication error indicator
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_update_onboarding_task(
    base_api_url,           # <fixture> str: root URL of hr-app API
    existing_onboarding_task,
    audit_log_client,
):
    task_id = existing_onboarding_task["id"]
    url = f"{base_api_url}/onboarding-tasks/{task_id}"

    payload = {"title": "Unauthorized Update Attempt"}
    response = requests.patch(url, json=payload, timeout=10)  # no auth header

    assert response.status_code == 401, (
        f"Expected 401 for unauthenticated request, got {response.status_code}"
    )

    body = response.json()
    assert "error" in body or "message" in body, "Response must contain error detail"

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=task_id, action="update")
    assert len(audit_entries) == 0, "Audit record must NOT be written for unauthenticated attempt"

tests.name: test_hr_admin_update_onboarding_task_status_state_transition_pending_to_completed
preconditions: A pre-existing onboarding-task exists with status='pending' (created via <fixture: pending_onboarding_task>)
expected: Task status in response body equals 'completed'
Audit record reflects prior state 'pending' and new state 'completed'
trace: R-WEB-026: audit record written on success
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_hr_admin_update_onboarding_task_status_state_transition_pending_to_completed(
    hr_admin_page,
    pending_onboarding_task,  # <fixture> returns task with status='pending'
    audit_log_client,
):
    task_id = pending_onboarding_task["id"]
    page = hr_admin_page

    page.goto(f"/onboarding-tasks/{task_id}/edit")
    current_status = page.input_value("[data-testid='task-status']")
    assert current_status == "pending", f"Pre-condition failed: status is '{current_status}'"

    page.select_option("[data-testid='task-status']", "completed")

    with page.expect_response(
        lambda r: f"/onboarding-tasks/{task_id}" in r.url
        and r.request.method in ("PUT", "PATCH", "POST")
    ) as resp_info:
        page.click("[data-testid='submit-btn']")

    response = resp_info.value
    assert response.status in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201, got {response.status}"
    body = response.json()
    assert body.get("status") == "completed", "Task status must be 'completed' after transition"

    expect(page.locator("[data-testid='success-toast']")).to_be_visible()

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=task_id, action="update")
    assert len(audit_entries) >= 1
    latest = audit_entries[-1]
    assert latest.get("previous_state") == "pending"
    assert latest.get("new_state") == "completed"
